programs.name: Access to Work (ATW)
programs.description: Standing order service for customers commuting to employment or job training sites. Trips are scheduled based on work start times so customers arrive on time.
programs.eligibility: Must use the program exclusively for travel to/from employment or job training. Must maintain a consistent weekly schedule.
programs.not_suitable_for: Persons with schedules that are different from day to day and week to week are better served by regular Next Day Access trips.
benefits: Trips scheduled to work start times for on-time arrival
Flexible modifications if work hours change (contact Standing Order department, no reapplication needed)
Standing Order service (no daily calling required)
programs.enrollment: Download application from accessla.org (English/Spanish) and submit

programs.name: Beyond the Curb (BTC)
programs.description: Origin-to-destination assistance where the driver accompanies the rider up to 60 feet from the vehicle. Available as a reasonable modification for riders who cannot navigate from curb to their destination independently.
programs.eligibility: Riders whose disability prevents them from using curb-to-curb paratransit for a specific trip. New applicants evaluated during initial eligibility screening. Existing customers apply via Reasonable Modification Request Form.
how_to_request: Request during trip reservation (for approved riders)
Call 800-827-0829, press #6 to request a Reasonable Modification Form
Grace period: current customers can request during reservations while applications are under review

scope.driver_requirement: Driver must maintain visual contact with vehicle

limitations: Driver cannot enter buildings, residences, or private doors
Not available in alleys, driveways, or gated communities
Driver may refuse if deemed unsafe
programs.important_note: Access is not a medical transportation service. Customers needing help beyond BTC should bring a Personal Care Attendant (rides free).

programs.name: Free Fare Program
programs.description: Certified Access riders can ride most fixed-route bus and rail systems in LA County for free using their green Access Rider ID Card as a TAP card.
programs.eligibility: All certified Access riders. No registration required. Does not affect paratransit eligibility.
programs.how_to_use: Present your green Access Rider ID Card to the TAP validator when boarding. Some agencies require showing the card to the driver.
programs.pca_note: The following systems charge fares for Personal Care Attendants: Beach Cities Transit, Culver City Bus, El Monte Trolley, Gardena Municipal Bus Line, LADOT DASH/Commuter Express, Long Beach Transit, Montebello Bus Lines, and Santa Clarita Transit.
metrolink_free_segments: Orange County/91 Line: to Norwalk/Santa Fe Springs
programs.metrolink_note: Requires reduced-rate ticket for out-of-county segments. Wheelchair users board at access ramp (top of platform), lower-level cars only.

programs.name: Parents with Disabilities Program (PWD)
programs.description: Assists Access-certified parents/guardians of children age 16 and younger with reliable transportation to and from school and school-related activities.
programs.eligibility: Access-certified riders who are parents or guardians of children 16 and younger
benefits: Trip pickup times based on school schedule to prevent tardiness
Linked trips: parent stays aboard after drop-off/pick-up to reach next destination
Minimum 30-minute intervals between same-day trips for school escort time
Up to 4 one-way same-day emergency trips monthly (9 AM to 7 PM)
Service hours: 6 AM to 9 PM, 7 days a week

enrollment.mail: Access Services, Parents with Disabilities Program, PO Box 5728, El Monte, CA 91734-1728

programs.name: Travel Training
programs.description: Free one-on-one individualized training that gives people with disabilities the skills required to travel safely on fixed-route public transportation.
programs.eligibility: All individuals who have completed the Access Services eligibility process. Participation does not affect your eligibility status.
skills_taught: Schedule interpretation for buses and trains
Signaling drivers for stops or assistance
programs.enrollment: Download Travel Training Application from accessla.org, submit via email, fax, or mail to MMP

programs.name: Reasonable Modification
programs.description: A modification to Access policies, practices, or procedures requested by an eligible customer to ensure they can use the paratransit system.
examples: Policy or procedure modifications for specific disability needs
how_to_request: Download Reasonable Modification Request Form from accessla.org
denial_criteria: Would fundamentally alter the nature of Access services, programs, or activities
Would create a direct threat to health or safety of others
Customer can fully use services without the modification
Would cause unreasonable financial or administrative burden
programs.complaint_process: File with Reasonable Modification Coordinator using same contact methods. Access reviews and responds within 14 calendar days.

contact.name: Torrance Johnson
contact.title: Reasonable Modification Coordinator
contact.mail: Access Services, ATTN: RMC, PO Box 5728, El Monte, CA 91734

programs.name: Visitor Program
programs.description: ADA paratransit service for visitors from outside Los Angeles County who cannot use accessible fixed-route transit due to disability-related functional limitations.
eligibility: Unable to use accessible fixed-route due to disability-related functional limitations
OR present ADA paratransit eligibility documentation from outside LA County
OR provide out-of-county residence proof and disability documentation
programs.duration: Up to 21 days within one calendar year (365 days from first trip). Travel days do not have to be consecutive.
programs.reapplication: After one-year expiration, visitor may reapply for another 21 days.
programs.exceeding_21_days: FTA regulations limit visitor paratransit to 21 days per calendar year. For longer stays, apply for local eligibility.

registration.documents_needed: Proof of eligibility from local paratransit provider OR proof of address outside LA County with disability documentation from medical provider

submit_via.mail: Access Services Customer Relations, PO Box 5728, El Monte, CA 91734

registration.processing_time: Within one business day from receipt

service_details.companions: Up to two individuals. PCA rides free, guests pay regular fare.
service_details.children: Free for ages 6 and under. Car seats required by law until age 8 or 4'9" tall.
service_details.luggage: Maximum two large grocery bags (25 pounds total per rider)

reservations.hours: Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM PDT

programs.inter_county_travel: Request Access send eligibility details to another county by calling 800-827-0829. Make request at least two days in advance. Paratransit providers allow up to 21 days per calendar year as visitors.
